name: Asparagus and Acorn Squash Rings
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim thick ends from asparagus stalks.
2. Cut each acorn squash in half crosswise and scoop out the seeds, discarding them.
3. Slice the squash into 1/4-inch rings.
4. Cook the squash rings and asparagus in boiling, salted water until tender. Do not overcook. Drain immediately after becoming tender.
5. Heat butter in a medium skillet. Add sugar and walnuts. Stir over medium heat until sugar is light brown.
6. Deglaze the pan with lemon juice, cider vinegar, and apple cider vinegar.
7. Cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture reduces by half.
8. To serve, layer 3 acorn squash rings on each of 4 plates. Place 4 asparagus spears through the acorn squash. Spoon sauce over.
NOTE: To make clarified butter, slowly melt a stick of butter over very low heat. Allow the solids to settle to the bottom; use the clear butter on top.
